compiled_state.title: As a developer, I get an accurate dso-config.conf generated from the conversation with contextual CI and project-aware defaults
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description

**What**: Generate dso-config.conf from the understanding built during the onboarding conversation, with contextual CI workflow logic, project-aware ticket prefix, and ACLI value suggestions
**Why**: Current project-setup suggests wrong commands and requires manual lookup of values that can be auto-detected or looked up
**Scope**:
- IN: Config generation from conversation state; ticket prefix derived from project name; CI workflow contextual prompting (offer examples when none exist, auto-populate from examples); ACLI_VERSION and ACLI_SHA256 auto-suggestion; all existing dso-config.conf key categories
- OUT: The conversation logic (S2); detection scripts (S1); design config keys (S5)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, dso-config.conf is generated from the understanding built during the onboarding conversation with all key categories populated
  ← Satisfies: "CI workflow configuration is contextually aware"
- When this story is complete, ticket prefix defaults derive from the project name (e.g., project 'my-app' suggests 'ma' or 'myapp')
  ← Satisfies: "Ticket prefix defaults derive from the project name"
- When this story is complete, when no GitHub Actions workflows exist, the skill does not prompt for workflow names — instead offers example workflows and auto-populates subsequent CI questions from those examples if accepted
  ← Satisfies: "when no GitHub Actions workflows exist, the skill offers example workflows"
- When this story is complete, ACLI_VERSION and ACLI_SHA256 are suggested automatically (via WebFetch of latest release or bundled defaults)
  ← Satisfies: "ACLI_VERSION and ACLI_SHA256 are suggested automatically"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] ACLI version lookup may fail if network unavailable — provide bundled fallback defaults
- [Testing] Config generation is deterministic given conversation state — testable with fixture inputs

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: All implementation tasks closed. Story complete: dso-config.conf generation with ticket prefix, CI examples, ACLI auto-suggestion.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
